verdict_reasoning: FN1 encodes fibronectin, a large ECM glycoprotein secreted as soluble plasma fibronectin or assembled into insoluble fibrillar ECM. It has no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor of its own. A cell-surface pool exists: fibronectin binds integrin receptors and heparan sulfate proteoglycans on the cell surface in a wash-resistant manner, and surface biotinylation / flow cytometry on intact cells confirms surface-associated fibronectin. However, the dominant biological form is secreted/matrix-deposited, not a stable TM-anchored precursor. Cell-state: matrix assembly is upregulated in wound healing and fibrosis but fibronectin is constitutively present on most cell surfaces. Tissue-restricted: no, broadly expressed. Lysosomal exocytosis: not documented. Dual-localization: PM pool alongside dominant secreted/ECM pool fits dual_localization but the surface attachment is via non-covalent integrin/HSPG binding. Stable_surface_attachment: fibronectin-integrin and fibronectin-syndecan interactions are largely wash-resistant in assembled fibrils. Best fit is 'stable_surface_attachment' under contextual, given the dominant secreted/ECM form but documented wash-resistant cell-surface pool enabling antibody and ADC approaches.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether fibronectin-integrin/HSPG surface attachment is truly wash-resistant in vivo vs. matrix-deposited form dominating; no dedicated TM anchor makes surface accessibility condition-dependent and cell-type-variable.
